entry.title: Updated Stay Certified available for SAP Sales Cloud Version 2
entry.content:
Hello learners!

On behalf of the SAP CX Product Learning team, I am pleased to inform you about the release of our SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 Stay Certified Assessment.

SAP Stay Certified provides you the opportunity to familiarize yourself with the latest updates and product changes and keep your certification valid. This Stay Certified program contains 

  • Webinar recording: SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 - Generative AI & Machine Learning - This material will help you learn about the recent enhancements in SAP Sales Cloud V2 to prepare for the assessment and stay certified.
  • A live session scheduled for May 15th 2025, another option provided as preparation material.
  • An assessment which checks your knowledge on the latest features. 

Please Note:

1. Stay Certified is accessible only to those who have been Certified on SAP Sales Cloud Version 2. If you haven't obtained your certification, please so do here:  https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-associate-implementation-consultant-sap-sales-cloud-version-2 

2. You will need an SAP Learning Hub  subscription to access Stay Certified as well as Certification.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: ABAP CDS Release News 2408 – External Entities
entry.content:
Hi @FlaviuBodea 

Currently, external entities are available in Cloud systems only and they can be used to connect to other public Cloud systems - SAP S/4HANA Cloud, SAP BTP ABAP environment, or SAP HANA Cloud.

The feature will most likely be available with the next big on premise shipment 2025.

Does that answer your question?

entry.title: FTP CPI Connectivity failed: SOCKS5 command failed with status: FORBIDDEN
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I’m setting up connectivity from SAP CPI to a local FTP server. The TCP connection from the Cloud Connector to the FTP server is working, and the connection is reachable.
The connectivity test from SAP CPI succeeds when "Check Directory Access" is off, but when enabled, I get the error: java.net.SocketException: SOCKS5 command failed with status: FORBIDDEN.

Similarly, when pushing a file through CPI interface, I receive:
org.apache.camel.component.file.GenericFileOperationFailedException: File operation failed: 229 Entering Extended Passive Mode (|||65087|) SOCKS5 command failed with status: FORBIDDEN.

I checked SAP Note 0003076681, which mentions ensuring passive mode is enabled and configuring the data port range. The FTP admin confirmed passive mode is enabled but couldn’t share the port range. I’ve only configured port 20 in the Cloud Connector, without a port range. What could be the issue? Do I need to configure a port range, or is there something else required?

Thanks and Regards,

Mohit

entry.title: How to tackle code adaptations for S/4HANA for IS-Health components?
entry.content:
Hi All!

I'm well aware that my question is rather wide but this is due to the "simplification" item for IS-Health impacting various different object types. I became aware about this fun item way back in 2019 when I ran a first S/4HANA readiness check on our systems. Some of the items flagged in our Z-code are fairly easy to correct but for some others, I'm not quite sure what an alternative in S/4HANA could be.

My hope is, that some of you have already gone through this "exercise" and can suggest some fixes we could apply in our code. I'm especially interested in these issues:

No longer available message classes --> "copy" to Z-messages or using another SAP-provided message class saying the same thing (but hopefully in a very basic class)? We use at least these messages:

  • MESSAGE i179(n8). "There is no data for your selection
  • MESSAGE s064(nf) WITH sy-subrc 'LT01'. "Error & while calling transaction &
  • MESSAGE e115(n2) WITH wa_kna1-kunnr. "Customer & was changed
  • MESSAGE s259(n1me). "Material document & & posted

No longer available function modules --> are there other SAP-routines we could use or should we copy the code to Z-function group / modules or perhaps put the needed logic directly in the affect code if there not many hits and the code is short & straightforward?

  • ISH_BAPIRET2_DISPLAY
  • ISH_POPUP_TO_CONFIRM_SIMPLE
  • FORMAT_DATE_4_OUTPUT

No longer available Memory IDs --> I'm not really familiar with these, which doesn't help with deciding how best to correct the impacted code! Any suggestions of whether creating Z-Memory-IDs is an option or if something completely different has to be used in its stead?

  • FIL
  • ANZ

Any takers?

Thanks much and Cheers

Bärbel

entry.title: Query by Bin internal process using Radio Frequency User Interface
entry.content:
Storage Bin:

A storage bin in SAP refers to a specific location within the warehouse where goods are stored. It is part of the warehouse structure and helps in organizing inventory for easy retrieval and management. SAP uses storage bins to optimize warehouse operations, ensuring efficient picking, putaway, and inventory control.

 

RFUI (Radio Frequency User Interface):


RFUI in SAP is the mobile interface used by warehouse workers to interact with the system via handheld devices. It provides real-time task instructions, such as picking, packing, and confirming activities, directly to workers on the floor. RFUI helps streamline operations and improve accuracy by reducing manual data entry.

 

Internal Process:

The internal process in SAP refers to the flow of goods and information within the warehouse, such as receiving, storing, and moving items between locations. These processes are designed to ensure that inventory is managed efficiently, and tasks are optimized. SAP supports internal processes with automation, enhancing overall operational efficiency and visibility.

  

Steps for Query by Bin internal process using Radio Frequency User Interface

 Step1:

Go to screen using the transaction code /n/scwm/rfui

Then navigate with the below process flow:

Internal processes-> 04 Stock/ Bin Query -> 01 Stock / Bin Query by Bin

 

 

 

Step2:

Provide the Storage Bin

Then click on bin detail

 

 The Bin details such as Storage type, Storage bin, Quantity and Unit of measure are displayed.

 

Conclusion:

Steps for Query by Bin internal process using Radio Frequency User Interface is provided

"The first 32 characters in the ASCII-table are unprintable control codes and are used to control peripherals such as printers." and there are some under my source data.
  • ASCII table - Table of ASCII codes, characters and symbols

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to process non-printable characters in SAP Data S...
entry.content:
Hello,

It is possible using the regex_replace function.

Example of mapping related to the column which might receive a non-printable characters:

  • regex_replace(<Input here your non-printable character/column>, '\\[\x00-\x09\x0B-\x0C\x0E-\x1F\\]', '')

For more information: regex_replace | SAP Help Portal

Best Regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – New options for managing Views for filters tables and charts
entry.content:
Hello @Jocelyn_Dart, 

nice blog as always 😄 
I totally get where you coming from with the idea of restricting the creation of public variants. 
But i also agree with @wagener-mark  and @Jack_Reynolds. Giving the permission to the "Adapt UI" for everyone who should create public variants is a big challenge. The Adapt UI Feature is prone to problems if not used correctly and for customers shifting to S/4 and getting used to Fiori it's an overkill for many people. So i already see the Fiori Admins doing all the work. I dont know if this is really the best way. Sure it's not a showstopper, but regarding the acceptance it's a downgrade given that we could easily manage ALV Layouts with S_ALV_LAYO authentication object.

A report like @wagener-mark said would be awesome.

entry.title: Re: How to process non-printable characters in SAP Data S...
entry.content:
Example of Job:

Let's use a File as source which contains unprintable characters generated from the Windows PowerShell:

Commands used (Windows PowerShell):

[char]0x07 | Set-Clipboard
[char]0x010 | Set-Clipboard
[char]0x013 | Set-Clipboard
[char]0x016 | Set-Clipboard
[char]0x018 | Set-Clipboard

Note: You can ignore "CF LF", since it is related to the line break.

This is how you enable the non-printable characters view under Notepad++:

In this job, the file previous mentioned will be our source and there will be a temp table as a target.
It is a simple job.

Under the Query, we will use the regex_replace function under the mapping of the desired column:

regex_replace(<input_string>, <regular expression pattern string>, <replacement string>, <regular expression processing flags>)

Link regarding the replacement string for non-printable characters ([\x00-\x09\x0B-\x0C\x0E-\x1F]): 

  • regex - How to remove what are apparently control characters in notepad++? - Super User

  • This is how you identify the non-printable characters under Notepad++:


Thank you,
SAP Support

entry.title: How to write in ClassificationClass.classificationAttributes collection?
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm putting hands on some custom logic that uses the method de.hybris.platform.catalog.model.classification.ClassificationClassModel#getClassificationAttributes to retrieve the list of classificationAttributeModels assigned to a specific ClassificationClass (Classifying Categories tab in the backoffice), what is the right way to populate this collection? I see that the attribute is writable=false inside catalog-items.xml:

<attribute qualifier="classificationAttributes" type="ClassificationAttributeList">
						<description>list of all attributes available within this class</description>
						<modifiers read="true" write="false" search="false" optional="true"/>
						<persistence type="jalo"/>
					</attribute>

So I was wondering what was the right way to populate this the ClassificationAttributeList collection, the only way I could insert data is using forceWrite=true via impex. Has someone experienced this?

 

Thank you

entry.title: Re: How to process non-printable characters in SAP Data S...
entry.content:
You can also use this code

 

form remove_invalid_control_chars
  changing
    text_to_strip.

  types:
    begin of control_char_list_struct,
      hex  type c,
      code type c length 3,
    end of control_char_list_struct.

  data:
    control_char_list       type table of control_char_list_struct,
    control_char_list_entry like line of control_char_list,
    test_string type string,
    white_space type string.

  white_space = cl_abap_conv_in_ce=>uccp( '0020' ).
  control_char_list = value #(
    ( hex = CL_ABAP_CONV_IN_CE=>uccp( '0000' ) code = 'NUL' )
    ( hex = CL_ABAP_CONV_IN_CE=>uccp( '0001' ) code = 'SOH' )
    ( hex = CL_ABAP_CONV_IN_CE=>uccp( '0002' ) code = 'STX' )
    ( hex = CL_ABAP_CONV_IN_CE=>uccp( '0003' ) code = 'ETX' )
    ( hex = CL_ABAP_CONV_IN_CE=>uccp( '0004' ) code = 'EOT' )
    ( hex = CL_ABAP_CONV_IN_CE=>uccp( '0005' ) code = 'ENQ' )
    ( hex = CL_ABAP_CONV_IN_CE=>uccp( '0006' ) code = 'ACK' )
    ( hex = CL_ABAP_CONV_IN_CE=>uccp( '0007' ) code = 'BEL' )
    ( hex = CL_ABAP_CONV_IN_CE=>uccp( '0008' ) code = 'BS' )
    ( hex = CL_ABAP_CONV_IN_CE=>uccp( '0009' ) code = 'HT' )
    ( hex = CL_ABAP_CONV_IN_CE=>uccp( '000A' ) code = 'LF' )
    ( hex = CL_ABAP_CONV_IN_CE=>uccp( '000B' ) code = 'VT' )
    ( hex = CL_ABAP_CONV_IN_CE=>uccp( '000C' ) code = 'FF' )
    ( hex = CL_ABAP_CONV_IN_CE=>uccp( '000D' ) code = 'CR' )
    ( hex = CL_ABAP_CONV_IN_CE=>uccp( '000E' ) code = 'SO' )
    ( hex = CL_ABAP_CONV_IN_CE=>uccp( '000F' ) code = 'SI' )
    ( hex = CL_ABAP_CONV_IN_CE=>uccp( '0010' ) code = 'DLE' )
    ( hex = CL_ABAP_CONV_IN_CE=>uccp( '0011' ) code = 'DC1' )
    ( hex = CL_ABAP_CONV_IN_CE=>uccp( '0012' ) code = 'DC2' )
    ( hex = CL_ABAP_CONV_IN_CE=>uccp( '0013' ) code = 'DC3' )
    ( hex = CL_ABAP_CONV_IN_CE=>uccp( '0014' ) code = 'DC4' )
    ( hex = CL_ABAP_CONV_IN_CE=>uccp( '0015' ) code = 'NAK' )
    ( hex = CL_ABAP_CONV_IN_CE=>uccp( '0016' ) code = 'SYN' )
    ( hex = CL_ABAP_CONV_IN_CE=>uccp( '0017' ) code = 'ETB' )

    ( hex = CL_ABAP_CONV_IN_CE=>uccp( '009F' ) code = 'APC' )
  ).

  loop at control_char_list into control_char_list_entry.
    replace all occurrences of control_char_list_entry-hex in text_to_strip with white_space.
  endloop.
endform.

 

Call the function with a text and you get the special chars replaced with a white space.

If you need to have \r and \n included in your text you can comment out the LF and CR entries.

entry.title: Destination usage in SAP BAS.
entry.content:
Hello!
I am trying to make a connection to a destination via SAP BAS app, that's a SAP FSM extension. Currently I can't trigger my destinations URL.
My destination:

This is my xs-app.json:

 

 

{
  "welcomeFile": "/index.html",
  "authenticationMethod": "route",
  "routes": [
    {
      "source": "^/equipmentUpdate/?(.*)",
      "target": "/$1",
      "destination": "EquipmentUpdate",
      "authenticationType": "none"

        "source": "^/resources/(.*)$",
        "target": "/resources/$1",
        "authenticationType": "none",

    },

        "source": "^(.*)$",
        "target": "$1",

        "authenticationType": "none"
    }
  ]
}

 

 

My mta.yaml:

entry.title: Artificial Intelligence and other intelligent ERP use case in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
Artificial Intelligence is reshaping industries by making businesses smarter, more efficient, and more adaptable. We at SAP are at the forefront of this transformation, empowering organizations to harness the full potential of artificial intelligence.

You certainly have read that SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is in general availability and will be release to our customers, for more check the release blog from @BeSchulze. In this blog, we will have a close look at the "intelligent technologies" like AI and machine learning that are at the heard of the Cloud ERP and will help our customer run better.

For your convenience, we created this product update video that will give you a summary of what's key in the "Intelligent ERP" product update with the release 2502.

Joule, our Natural Language, GenAI Co-Pilot

Joule is the AI copilot for the business. Joule’s AI agents perform autonomous tasks and work together through workflows across all domains of your business including supply chain, procurement, and finance to deliver connected, enterprise-wide business outcomes.

I am happy to announce that Joule is now generally available. With intuitive interaction, Joule transforms complex tasks into simple, efficient actions, offering instant insights, quick navigation, and guidance when needed.

Joule’s card creation feature in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 offers quick, customized snapshots of important data—like purchase orders or delivery updates—right where you need them. Not only does this save time, it also offers a great user experience!

AI-assisted UX

The role of User Experience is critical for a Cloud ERP,  it is playing a crucial role in how customers engage with and build trust in the software. In this release as well, AI is brining our Cloud ERP user experience to the next level.

Smart summarization and easy filter are here to facilitate your tasks like never before! Smart summarization leveraged generative AI to summarize object pages within SAP Fiori elements-based applications. Users can review, edit, and copy these summaries into productivity tools for efficient follow-ups. It improves business data understanding, saves time, and delivers trustworthy results with easy editing. Capabilities include section selection for summarization, a structured output with a rich text editor for edits, and seamless integration for follow-ups. 

AI-powered smart summarization streamlines workflows and enhances efficiency by transforming complex data into clear and concise summaries.

Easy filter simplifies the process of filtering list reports of SAP Fiori elements-based applications using natural language. It harnesses the power of generative AI to streamline query inputs, providing a more intuitive experience than traditional manual filtering methods. Users can conveniently access recent and favorite queries. The innovation improves business data understanding, saves time when filtering based on known criteria, and ensures trustworthy AI results. It supports sophisticated filtering criteria and allows recent filters to be marked as favorites.

AI-driven easy filtering simplifies data exploration and minimizes effort.

These features were shipped through a beta testing in last release and are now generally available with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502.

Beta shipments are a great way of testing innovations and enhancements early on. With this release, we ship three more use cases through beta testing:

  • AI-assisted easy enterprise search: This tool enables natural language search capabilities within SAP Fiori launchpad, powered by generative AI. It streamlines the search for business objects, offering a more intuitive, efficient user experience. Key features include natural language support in the search field, adjustable settings for enabling this capability at the end-user level, and the indication of active natural language support within the search interface.
  • AI-assisted error explanation: The AI-assisted error explanation tool enhances users' understanding of errors in SAP Fiori elements-based applications, providing recommendations and solutions through generative AI. It integrates directly with error visualization, enabling one-click action to generate explanations. The innovation saves time, offers better insights into errors, and fosters an intuitive user experience. It presents a well-structured explanation containing error details and recommended solutions.
  • AI-assisted personalization of My Home: The smart personalization feature allows users to use natural language through generative AI to add insights cards to their My Home experience. It facilitates quick and easy customization of My Home with efficiently generated insights cards, previewed before addition to ensure data quality. It supports a variety of SAP Fiori elements-based apps as the basis for insights card creation, providing a time-saving, intuitive user experience with reliable AI results.

Finance and Risk Innovations leveraging Joule

Finance is the backbone of every company: investing, reporting, budgeting, saving,... in all those domains, Joule can help business user making the different processes seamless, more efficient, more reliable.

As you remember, I’ve shown you two use cases in beta shipments in my last product update. I am happy to share that the financial business insights into the cost center review booklet as well as the configuration for US tax jurisdictions are now generally available!

Extended Situations in the To Do section of My Home help manage complex tasks by providing AI-powered insights and actionable alerts. This feature streamlines task management by integrating situation handling, keeping you informed and focused without switching between lists and emails.

 

In this release, we help you stay updated on business issues, and make quick, informed decisions with Joule!

The integration of SAP's AI copilot Joule with Situation Handling enables users to receive up-to-date information on demand. Joule displays relevant issues, shares additional information, and directs users to the appropriate apps, ensuring a continuous overview of responsibilities while working on their daily tasks.

And you can now streamline decision-making with targeted recommendations using genAI!

Generative AI enhances Situation Handling by providing targeted recommendations to streamline decision-making. The extended My Situations app generates AI-driven solutions based on data context from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and external data via a chosen large language model (LLM). This integration leverages SAP Business Technology Platform for intelligent, context-aware recommendations, improving issue resolution efficiency.

Machine Learning Scenario

SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management on SAP BTP provides a unified solution for receiving and managing supplier invoices, with connection to multiple systems, such as SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. With this release, a brand-new machine learning scenario adds efficiencies in this process.

Document information extraction templates in Central Invoice Management enable users to create supplier-specific templates for accurate data extraction from invoices. By correcting extraction errors and training the machine learning model, these templates improve accuracy for future invoices with similar layouts. This ensures higher confidence in data extraction, reducing manual adjustments and enabling seamless invoice posting in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Customize extraction templates to improve accuracy for supplier-specific invoice layouts and ensure precise data extraction.

Interested in more innovation in the release update? Read the other product updates and watch the other videos in this playlist where my team of product experts cover selected lines of business and industries.

 

📽️ Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!  

Between January 21 and January 24, we conducted a series of 21 engaging live sessions to showcase the thrilling innovations included in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release. If you missed the live sessions, don't worry! You can still access the whole series or select the sessions that interest you the most. Seize this opportunity to watch them on demand at your convenience–anywhere, anytime. 

🔎Find All of Our Assets  

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

Click to open the SAP BTP Enablement Wheel

entry.title: RBP: How to deal with changing assignment of positions - job codes
entry.content:
We are looking for some best practice guidance on RBP tied to positions and job codes.  Our team does not have much experience with clients controlling RBP at the position/job code level.  Forvia is running into issues.

 

Here is the problem:
When HR users get access to SF, it's tied to their position. This works well for managing access - when someone leaves or changes positions, their access is automatically removed. The new person who takes over that position automatically gets the same access.

But there's a catch: If the job code or scope of the position changes later, the automatic permissions might not match what the role actually needs anymore. This means people could end up with incorrect access levels.

 

Is there any best practice or suggestion on how to handle this?  Have you seen this before with other clients and if so, how do they approach it?  Can we setup an alert? Is there any solution we can put into place?

entry.title: Re: Passing values in Input Parameters - Variables of Datasphere Model from Power BI
entry.content:
@Jinal_Patel 

I found a problem...

A Single Analytic model is deployed as multiple column view in the database:

1) A column View with base fact fields -> that can be read from DB users.

2) A column view for every associated dimension. -> that can not be read from DB users.

3) A final column view (Fact join Dimensions). -> that can not be read from DB users.

example with a database analysis user (read all permission).

Example with a DB User created inside a space (2 and 3 are missing!!):

entry.title: Re: Combining Multiple Wildcard Searches in Same Report
entry.content:
You are not looking for a row that matches all of those patterns so just add each of your conditions with an OR instead of an AND.  There is no reason to group them.  You want:

   Inventorynumber matches pattern "AB%"
OR Inventorynumber matches pattern "CD%"
OR inventorynumber matches pattern "EF%%"

entry.title: Retail, Fashion and Vertical Business in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
1. General Availability (2502)

SAP is proud to announce the general availability of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, retail, fashion, and vertical business solution, marking a pivotal step forward in delivering tailored enterprise resource planning (ERP) capabilities for retailers of all sizes. Built specifically to address the complexities of the retail industry, this solution enables seamless end-to-end integration of processes, providing the operational agility required in today’s fast-paced retail environment.

Retailers have often been constrained by fragmented solutions that excel in isolated areas but fail to deliver the integrated experience needed for cohesive retail operations. The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition bridges this gap by offering an ERP system designed with retail-specific capabilities to support merchandising, store operations, and complex supply chain management.

Built on the flexibility of public cloud architecture, the platform combines industry-specific processes, unified data, and embedded AI, ensuring a comprehensive suite of tools for finance, procurement, merchandising, and customer experience management. This new offering provides retailers with the tools to streamline processes, deliver personalized experiences, and drive sustainable growth, all while leveraging out-of-the-box integrations for faster time to value. With SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, retailers can now achieve the agility and innovation needed to thrive in today’s digitally driven marketplace.

 

2. Solution Overview

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, retail, fashion, and vertical business provides comprehensive end-to-end process coverage, ensuring seamless operations across all roles and functions within a retail enterprise. This solution integrates key areas such as master data management, pricing and promotions, demand and supply planning, procurement, supply chain management, store commerce, and sales. For example, it enables master data specialists to manage assortments and product taxonomies while providing merchandise planners with tools for pricing and promotion strategies. Additionally, purchasers and supply chain specialists benefit from streamlined procurement and inventory management processes, including inbound and outbound logistics. Store associates and managers can handle in-store inventory, customer orders, and connectivity with ease, ensuring an efficient store-level operation. The solution also supports billing clerks in managing accounts receivable and sales processes, including omnichannel order fulfillment. With optional capabilities like retail logistics with third-party warehouses and seasonal planning, SAP S/4HANA Cloud delivers a flexible, scalable platform to drive operational efficiency, enhance customer experiences, and support strategic growth for retailers.

 

3. Roadmap & Localization:

Full Roadmap for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, retail, fashion, and vertical business: SAP Road Map Explorer

In 2025, the feature roadmap for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, retail, fashion, and vertical business highlights significant advancements designed to empower retailers with enhanced capabilities. Release 2502 includes features such as fast and intuitive order capturing via simplified Fiori apps, in-store merchandise management enhancements, rapid replenishment improvements, and advanced integrations with industry cloud applications for real-time inventory synchronization. It also introduces flexible business roles, granular authorization capabilities, and value-added services for foundational retail operations.

Looking ahead to Release 2508, the roadmap emphasizes enabling AI-driven insights and extensibility, including support for hybrid orders, enhanced assortment management with AI co-pilot integration, and advanced intercompany sales and stock transfers. These developments reflect SAP's focus on equipping retailers with tools to streamline processes, improve operational efficiency, and meet evolving customer expectations.

 

The localization roadmap for SAP S/4HANA Cloud ensures the solution's global relevance by expanding its availability to key markets. The 2502 release supports localization as a self-service in ten additional countries, including India, Australia, Mexico, Singapore, the UK, and Belgium, among others. This builds on the already robust localization support in markets such as Germany, France, the US, and Canada. The roadmap for future releases plans to further broaden the geographic reach, targeting additional countries in Europe, Asia, and South America. By addressing local tax, legal, and market-specific requirements, SAP S/4HANA Cloud ensures retailers worldwide can leverage its capabilities with confidence, fostering global adoption and operational consistency.

 

 4. 2502 Release Highlights

 

Fast and Easy Sales Order Capturing in Stores

Customers buying high-value goods from a retail store need an invoice for warranty or return purpose. Also they want to pay and pick-up the goods immediately or visit the store later for pick-up. Store Associate needs a very simple app to capture articles, calculate the total price and complete the transaction including goods movement in the system , in a very short time. Mange In-store sales order app provides the feature of single click generation of logistic documents.

Value Proposition:

  • Quickly and intuitively meet customers' requests in the stores
  • Support the sales order process for goods that need to be procured from a distribution center or a supplier, which is a must for the segments consumer electronics, DIY (do-it-yourself), specialty retail, or fashion retail
  • Increase customer retention and revenue

Capabilities:

  • Streamlined user interface for fast and intuitive maintenance
  • Determination of order type, sales organization, and distribution channel
  • Determination of the store
  • Display of the gross price at the header and item levels
  • Follow-on document creation in one click

For more information, see: Manage In-store Sales Orders

 

Efficient Assortment Management and Listing

Assortments are collections of articles for the purpose of defining which articles are relevant for which sites (stores and DCs). In particular, they are required to check whether an article can be procured by a site. In addition, assortments play an important role in other processes like Point of Sale outbound communication.

Assortments can be defined in terms of assortment modules that serve as article containers, which then are assigned to sites. As a result of listing assortment information is made available for operational usage in procurement. Analysis capabilities enriched by KPIs provide the needed transparency which articles are allocated to which sites, and serve as the basis for decision making processes. 

Value Proposition:

  • Enhance revenue streams by strategically curating assortments
  • Reduce operations costs by providing store managers correct information on what articles they are allowed to order, store and sell
  • Streamline the process of assigning articles to assortments
  • Assure optimal product placement across stores and distribution centers while minimizing markdowns and write-offs

Capabilities:

  • Elaborate assortment analyses to make sure the right articles are allocated to the right locations
  • Insights into KPIs with a user-friendly interface
  • APIs for events, assortments maintenance and DC listing

For more information, see: Assortment Planning and Management

 

Enhancements of In-Store Apps

The way how store associates and managers execute relevant processes differs significantly from employees working in the headquarters. Complex user interfaces are not an option for in-store processes because of costly trainings, enforced by high fluctuation rates of store personnel. The in-store Fiori apps are user friendly, self-explanatory and streamlined for in-store processes. It enables store associates to: 

  • Transfer article (from store to store/distribution center)
  • Perform a planned or unplanned physical inventory count
  • Adjust the stock quantity for different reasons such as shrinkage, damage

Value Proposition:

  • Streamline store operations for all article types
  • Support retailers of segments that require any of the specific article types listed below
  • Reduce inventory carrying cost by efficiently managing inventory and stock movements to reduce obsolescence and shrinkage costs

Capabilities:

  • Enhancing in-store apps to enable comprehensive support for:
  • Full articles with assigned empty articles (typically to handle deposits)
  • Structured articles, such as sales sets
  • Weight articles, for which the base unit of measure is weight

For more information, see: In-Store Merchandise and Inventory Management

 

Value-Added Services for Fashion Retailers

In some scenarios it is required to associate additional activities to the actual product as part of the buying or selling process. That can either be physical products (like a hanger for a coat, or a price label), or services (like ironing, or special packaging). These are called value-added services (VAS), as they increase the value of the product for the recipient.

Relevant VAS information can be created in master data maintenance.
During order processing, VAS related information can be entered in the respective document (purchase order, stock transfer order, and sales order). From there, VAS information is then transferred to the follow-on documents, such as outbound delivery, inbound delivery. This enables personnel (for example a warehouse clerk) to carry out the special tasks needed before delivering the goods to the recipient.

Value Proposition:

  • Increases the value of the delivered merchandise for the goods recipient by including necessary services
  • Enhance operational efficiency by providing needed information to personnel so specific tasks can be executed

Capabilities:

  • Maintenance of value-added services
  • Definition and maintenance of VAS information in operational documents, such as purchase order, stock transfer order, sales order
  • Availability of VAS information in logistic execution documents, such as inbound and outbound deliveries, to enable personnel to execute the needed tasks

For more information, see Value-Added Services Management

 

5. Further Information

  • Official product page: SAP website | Partner portal
  • Full retail solution processes: Process Navigator – SAP for Me
  • Product assistance on SAP Help
  • Learning journey: Exploring SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, retail, fashion and vertical business
  • End-2-End Retail Demo: Interactive Value Journey
  • Partner portal:
  • Hands-on Tutorial: Content Discovery -  E2E Retail Demos

entry.title: Outbound EDI Customer Invoice SupplierPartyID inconsistency
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I have configured the "Customer Invoice - Send (B2B)" API to send EDI messages when a customer invoice is completed. (https://api.sap.com/api/CO_EDI_INVOICE_REQUEST_OUT/resource)

The message is sent but there are inconsistency in it, in the "Party" nodes.

Example:

For example, my billing document has those Business Partners:

==>The Sold-to, Bill-to and Payer is the customer: "Client FR 1 (CUSTFR1)"

The Supplier is the Company (FR10).

Outbound message:

The "SupplierPartyIDs I receive in the outbound message in the Party part is:

 

 

<Party PartyType="BillFrom">
      <SupplierPartyID>CUSTFR1</SupplierPartyID>
      ...
    </Party>
    <Party PartyType="SoldTo">
      <SupplierPartyID>CUSTFR1</SupplierPartyID>
      ...
    </Party>
    <Party PartyType="BillTo">
      <SupplierPartyID>CUSTFR1</SupplierPartyID>
      ...
    </Party>
    <Party PartyType="Payer">
      <SupplierPartyID>CUSTFR1</SupplierPartyID>
      ...
    </Party>
    <Party PartyType="ShipTo">
      <SupplierPartyID>CUSTFR1</SupplierPartyID>
      ...
    </Party>

 

 

All SupplierPartyIDs are the customer's, but the BillFrom type Party's should be the company's.

 

I think it may be something missing in the configuration, but I don't know what. 

Has someone an idea of what can be missing ?

 

Thank you for your help,

Marion Marolany

entry.title: Unable to Block or Delete the Service Agent who is Also an Internal Employee
entry.content:
Unable to block or delete a Service Agent since he/she is also an Internal Employee. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Unable to Block or Delete the Service Agent who is Al...
entry.content:
It is not possible to block a service agent as there is no specific role and an internal employee always needs to be active until he/she gets terminated in Personnel Administration work center. So if an Employee has been in any point of time an internal Employee, the status of the Business Partner cannot be changed.

The status is persisted at business partner level and there is no business partner role specific status.

If the same Business Partner instance is an internal and external Employee at once, the status can't be changed anymore. The reason is that for internal Employees the status has to be always = 'Active'.

Special Case: The Service Agent is Active even after the Internal Employee is Terminated

Even though you have terminated the employee in Personnel Administration work center, the respective Business Partner remains Active because as per definition, a business partner can be used for other roles like Account, Suppliers, etc. Blocking a Business Partner while terminating an employee will result in inconsistency, if the Business partner is used in any other Business transaction document (for example: sales order, purchase order).

Although the Employee is terminated, the system only allows the status active for internal Employees. The reason for this design is consumer of internal Employees (Rehire Employee, Employee OWL, Change workplace address, Change Private Address, etc.) counting on this system behavior. Hence, it is not possible to block/Obsolete a Business Partner which is an Internal Employee (Active or Terminated).

In cases, if the Business partner has only Service Agent role, you can block the status manually in the Business Partner Data work center by using Change Status. So if a Service Agent has been in any point of time an internal Employee, the status cannot be changed.

You can also refer to KBA: https://me.sap.com/notes/1645825

entry.title: Re: Failed to execute 'send' on 'XMLHttpRequest'
entry.content:
Dear @Jacekkp111 

I looked through your code and I have a few words to say:

  1. Please! For the love of god and your company, do NOT use string concatenation to insert the altID into your URL without validating the content. Or else you have yourself a wonderfull Code Injection. Use the JS tools to remove HTML character like & and single quotes to prevent a SQL Injection.
  2. The part that could be the reason for the crash of your problem could be the inclusion of whitespaces in your URL. As the error message explains, it fails to load the URL.
  3. The URL in the image of the error message and the image/text of your code do not match. I am not talking about the top level domain part, but about the GET parameter. The error message image shows
    $filter=HandlingUnitExtermalID%20eq%20%27000000008%27
    while the text and the image of the code show
    $filter=Warehouse eq '1050' and EWHndlgUnitAltvID eq '" + altID + "'"
    The %20 is the HTML replacement for whitespace while %27 is a single quote.

So to resolve your issue I suggest using a try catch block around the send() function and dig through the stack trace because I don't think it has to do with auth credentials but with the way you construct your URL.

Also please make sure to insert your code in a proper format. The editor of the SAP community forum has a code tag. It makes reading the code much easier 😉

@Giuseppe432Can you please post your code? Or better yet open a new question so we don't have 2 issues within one post 🙂

Kind regards radinator

entry.title: Re: How to show a busy indicator in story while the table...
entry.content:
To the best of my knowledge there's no api/scripting way to flag when refresh has completed,
however if refresh times are consistent you could use a timer to hide a message

something like this in the page 'oninitialisation event'

Application.showBusyIndicator("I'm really busy right now refreshing all your wonderful data");
Timer.start(3);

In the timer on timeout, chuck this code in:    Application.hideBusyIndicator();

entry.title: Re: Activate Open Item Management for G/L Accounts not ha...
entry.content:
Yes this is the correct design 

as referenced in the help documentation 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/c8d4d165bdd24fbf9adf0a061966c19a.html?locale=en-US

First, you schedule the job in your development system. You can find the job template Activate Open Item Management for G/L Accounts in the Schedule Accounting Data Changes app. When the job runs in the development system, the indicator is set in the G/L account master data, and the related journal entry line items are adjusted. This G/L account is also marked for conversion in your test and your production system.

You then need to update your G/L account master data and journal entry line items in your test system. To do this, run the job in your test system. Afterwards, run the job in your production system.

This three-step approach ensures that the changes to the journal entry line items in your test and your production system are initiated only by users with the authorizations to do so.

entry.title: Webinar on the Danish bookkeeping act
entry.content:
The recently enacted Danish accounting law implements various requirements that affect the applications used by companies for bookkeeping.

Join the webinar where SAP and PwC will provide essential guidance and key steps to meet these requirements. They will also discuss the effects on both standard and customized bookkeeping applications. Visitors will be able to ask experts questions.

SAP will provide an update on the new product features tailored for the Danish market. They will also explain how their SAP systems adhere to the new bookkeeping legislation for non-registered systems and discuss their roadmap for future deliveries.

Speaker: Ingjerd Aamodt, Localization Product Manager, SAP Globalization.

The webinar will be conducted in English.

You can find more information about the webinar here. 

To secure your spot, please register here.
